artPrompt: A gaunt cleric in ashen robes bows over a dying ember of a candle, calmly closing a blank ledger with bone-white hands, a cold-gold halo glinting at his hooded crown. Guttering candles surround him in the dark; a looming clock tower fades into the blood-dark sky behind. One solemn light source isolates his downturned face, smoke curling upward, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Last Rites Clerk
text:
When Last Rites Clerk enters the battlefield, each player discards a card. Get your affairs in order.
Whenever another creature dies, you may pay {1}. If you do, draw a card and put a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock.
flavorText: Files the paperwork before the screaming even stops. Very efficient.
